title: viola silvatica subsp. nemoralis
intro: Viola silvatica subsp. nemoralis, also known as the woodland violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the violet genus (Viola). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Viola silvatica subsp. nemoralis include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support respiratory health. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve using its leaves, flowers, and roots in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in topical applications such as salves and ointments.
